meta_title: For Students: client workflow to Solve can't finish projects (Intermediate Checklist)
meta_description: Learn how to client workflow and overcome can't finish projects. A Intermediate-level Checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: Client Workflow to Solve Project Inertia
content.seo_description: Stop abandoning projects. Master an intermediate client-based workflow using Antigravity and Claude Code to ship your first real-world AI application today.
content.answer_block: The root cause of unfinished projects is a lack of defined client constraints. By adopting a professional 'Client-First' workflow, you treat your personal projects like paid contracts. This forces scope control, iterative delivery, and measurable milestones, transforming chaotic coding sessions into structured, shippable software releases that build your developer portfolio.
content.intro:
You sit down at 2:00 AM, fueled by caffeine and ambition, ready to build the next big thing. By 4:00 AM, the codebase is a tangled mess of half-baked features, and the spark is gone. You are staring at another 'abandoned' folder on your desktop, a graveyard of lost potential that hurts your resume more than it helps.

But the gap between a student and a senior engineer isn't talent; it's a workflow. When you switch your mindset from 'hacking' to 'servicing a client,' you gain the guardrails needed to ship. We are going to turn your next project into a professional delivery using Antigravity and Claude Code to maintain velocity from commit one to deployment.

steps.title: Define the Client Deliverable
steps.description: Stop building 'apps' and start building 'solutions.' Define a singular, high-value feature your client needs.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Act as a Lead Product Manager. Create a 3-point requirement document for a project that solves [Insert Problem] in under 48 hours of development time.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'create a project spec file based on these 3 requirements'

steps.title: Establish the Architecture Blueprint
steps.description: Before coding, define the data flow to ensure you don't get stuck in refactoring loops.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Design a clean, modular architecture for this project. Focus on separating the LLM integration layer from the frontend logic.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'generate the directory structure and boilerplate code based on the architecture blueprint'

steps.title: Rapid Prototyping with Claude Code
steps.description: Use Claude Code to handle the boilerplate, allowing you to focus on the core logic.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write the core business logic function for [Feature] with strict type safety and error handling.
steps.claude_code_command: claude add 'implement core business logic for user authentication and data processing'

steps.title: Implement the Feedback Loop
steps.description: Simulate a client review every 4 hours to ensure you are meeting the initial requirements.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Review the current codebase against the original project spec. Identify any 'scope creep' and suggest how to prune it.
steps.claude_code_command: claude review 'check for scope creep and suggest code cleanup'

steps.title: Final Polish and Deployment
steps.description: Package the project for production, ensuring documentation is automated.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a professional README.md that highlights the problem solved, the tech stack, and a 'How to Run' guide.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'generate production documentation and prepare for deployment'

pro_tips: Always commit your code before using Claude Code to make large changes; it makes rolling back trivial.
Treat every bug as a 'client request' for a fix, not a personal failure.
Limit your project scope to one core feature; you can always add 'version 2.0' later.
Use Antigravity to explain complex dependencies before you install them to avoid 'dependency hell'.
If you feel stuck for more than 30 minutes, force a commit and take a 15-minute walk.
content.next_steps: Once you finish your first project using this workflow, deploy it to Vercel or Railway and add a link to your LinkedIn profile. Use the 'Client-First' framework for your next project, but increase the complexity by 20%.
content.conversion_block: Stop letting unfinished projects sit in your Git history. Master the workflow that separates professional engineers from hobbyists. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
